what_it_does: Documents an AI-harvested set of candidate generative design rules from substrate sources, organized by design dimension with ratio/value data, evidence class, and source addresses, plus an excluded provenance section and harvest summary.
observations: States that the rules are leads rather than verified law and that human crystallization is still required.
Organizes rules under space, proportion, alignment, type-scale, color, texture, motion, composition, voice, and meta-principle.
Each rule entry generally includes a generative statement, ratio_or_value, evidence class, and source address.
Preserves two independent base-unit witnesses for spacing rather than collapsing them into one value.
Keeps opposite responsive spacing directions separate: structural spacers shrink on mobile while content-block gaps grow on mobile.
Includes one excluded palette rule marked keep=false for provenance.
Ends with a harvest summary describing dimension density, absent items, and five named spatial-grammar candidates.
standouts: Strong emphasis on rule-driven generation, token resolution, scope, cascade, and context-derived values.
Spacing is treated as a tokenized system with base-unit multiples, semantic tokens, and role-dependent responsive behavior.
Composition is framed through scope, address paths, role-to-value resolution, connectors, registries, and reusable unit primitives.
The file explicitly separates reusable generative rules from aesthetic palette specifics.
conventions: Uses markdown headings for dimensions and bullet-list rule records.
Uses bold rule statements followed by nested metadata bullets.
Marks evidence as Observed or Inferred.
Keeps provenance as filesystem-style source addresses with anchors.
Uses ratio_or_value as a compact field for numeric values, ratios, state vocabularies, and relational formulas.
concerns: The file mixes harvested rule inventory, interpretation, provenance notes, exclusions, and summary synthesis in one document.
Some entries are explicitly interpretive while others are observed, so consumers need to preserve the evidence class.
The ratio_or_value field carries heterogeneous data types, including numbers, formulas, lists, semantic mappings, and prose.
notes: Brief this as a provenance-preserving candidate rule ledger for generative visual/design grammar, not as a ratified specification.
questions: What process will promote a candidate rule from this harvest into a ratified grammar rule?
Should inferred unifiers remain in the same ledger as observed source statements or be separated into an interpretation layer?
Should ratio_or_value be normalized into typed subfields for values, formulas, ranges, and vocabularies?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it presents candidate harvested rules with provenance and cautions that they are not verified law.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to preserve raw design-rule evidence and early interpretation so a later human or system can crystallize a reusable generative grammar without losing source context or boundary cases.
novelty: The file treats visual design rules and architecture rules as one substrate ledger, with ratios, scope resolution, cascade behavior, and semantic tokens recorded in the same structure.
contribution: Contributes a structured inventory of candidate generative rules, preserving source addresses, evidence class, numeric ratios, interpretive unifiers, exclusions, and summary signals that a fused system could retain as provenance-rich design grammar material.
summary_for_embedding: A markdown ledger of candidate generative design rules harvested from substrate sources, grouped by design dimension and annotated with ratio_or_value, Observed/Inferred class, source provenance, exclusions, and harvest summary.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to carry forward provisional design-rule evidence and interpretation for later human crystallization into a reusable grammar.
